scope: Coupled real-d02 warmed per-step timing, base 4e3a9ff vs Thompson-opt (sed unroll=2 default, bit-identical). Harness: proofs/perf/warmed_timing.py (dt=10s, physics ON, guards ON, fp64, radiation cadence 180). NOTE: warmed marginal is noisy (compile-excluded, host-timer); same harness both sides = apples-to-apples.
verdict: Thompson default change (sed unroll=2, BIT-IDENTICAL) gives ~1.05x at the coupled step -> ~5.3x clean / ~7.8x realistic (was 5.06x/7.5x). >=10x NOT reached by faithful-explicit Thompson optimization; the >1.5x Thompson lever (implicit sedimentation, ~2.4x kernel) is gated on a precipitating oracle + coupled skill (scheme change / ADR).
